sql_array: CREATE TABLE factorial_test (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, value INTEGER, description TEXT);

INSERT INTO factorial_test (id, value, description) VALUES (1, 5, 'Factorial of a prime number'), (2, 3, 'Factorial of a small number'), (3, NULL, 'NULL case'), (4, -1, 'Factorial of a negative number');
SELECT id, value, factorial(value) AS factorial_value FROM factorial_test WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, value, factorial(value) AS factorial_value FROM factorial_test WHERE value > 0 GROUP BY value HAVING factorial(value) < 100;
SELECT id, value, CASE WHEN value IS NULL THEN 'NULL Input' ELSE CAST(factorial(value) AS VARCHAR) END AS result FROM factorial_test;
SELECT f.id, f.value, f2.value AS join_value, factorial(f.value) AS factorial_value FROM factorial_test f JOIN factorial_test f2 ON f.value < f2.value;
SELECT id, value, factorial(value) + 5 AS adjusted_factorial FROM factorial_test WHERE value > 0 ORDER BY adjusted_factorial;
SELECT id, value, factorial(value) AS factorial_from_operator FROM factorial_test WHERE value = 6 HAVING 6! = factorial(6);
SELECT id, value, factorial(IFNULL(value, 0)) AS null_handling_factorial FROM factorial_test;
SELECT id, value, 4! AS factorial_of_four FROM factorial_test WHERE value IS NOT NULL AND value < 10;
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE temp_factorial AS SELECT id, value, factorial(value) AS computed_factorial FROM factorial_test;
SELECT id, value FROM temp_factorial WHERE computed_factorial > 10 ORDER BY computed_factorial DESC;
SELECT id, value, factorial(value + 2) AS adjusted_factorial FROM factorial_test WHERE value IN (SELECT value FROM factorial_test WHERE value IS NOT NULL);
